rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Defer Pltf Deposition Until She Is Medically Able To Undergo That Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARCIA GIUSTI'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Defer Plaintiff Deposition Until She Is Medically Able To Undergo That Process Pro Tem Judge Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The plaintiff will not be required to submit to a deposition at this time. However, the plaintiff's deposition will commence in 30 days and will be completed within the next 60 days in one or multiple sittings, with each deposition session lasting no more than 2 hours per session. If the plaintiff refuses to submit to the deposition to completion as stated within the next 60 days, the plaintiff will submit to neuropsychological testing by a doctor of defendant's choice at a location no further than 75 miles from the plaintiff's residence, for the purpose of determining whether the plaintiff is medically capable of submitting to a deposition lasting less than 2 hours per session. The cost of the testing will be borne by the plaintiff, not to exceed $3,000. If the doctor determines that the deposition can proceed in sessions of no more than 2 hours, that deposition is to be completed within 30 days of the date of the medical report. If the report states that the plaintiff is not able to be deposed in sessions lasting no more than 2 hours, then the deposition will proceed, or will not proceed, based on the findings of that doctor. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, and sustained, without leave to amend, as to the third and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege causes of action for defamation and slander, tortious interference with business advantage, and trespass. For the first cause of action, although Defendant Nolting's alleged defamatory statements to law enforcement are barred by the litigation privilege, Plaintiff has pled other defamatory statements that are not barred. A demurrer tests the sufficiency of an entire cause of action, and not part of a cause of action. For the third cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress, and Plaintiff has not demonstrated that he can do so if leave to amend is granted. For the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled a cognizable cause of action, and Plaintiff has not demonstrated that he can do so if leave to amend is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Expenses. Pursuant to the Court's Order Granting Defendant Wah-Bun Lee's Motion to Compel Production of Documents, filed March 21, 2013, the Court imposes a monetary discovery sanction in the amount of $4972.10. (See CCP §§ 2031.320; 2023.030(a).) This amount is based on defense counsels' reasonable fees ($4000) and costs ($972.10) in connection with the filing and opposition of the motion to compel, and this motion, and is to be paid to Defendant Wah-Bun Lee and imposed only against Plaintiff's counsel Mr. Mutalipassi. (See CCP §2031.320 (monetary sanction imposed against an attorney who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel compliance without substantial justification); CCP §2023.030(a).) By its March 21, 2013 Order, the Court has found that Plaintiff's counsel unsuccessfully opposed the motion to compel production without substantial justification. The only remaining issue to be determined was the amount of the discovery sanction. Plaintiff's counsel shall pay Defendant consistent with this order within 30 days from entry of this order. The request for sanctions under CCP §128.7 is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALLISON JOHNSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue Or In The Alternative
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO's Motion To Transfer Venue. The motion to transfer to San Mateo County is granted. San Mateo has the absolute right to remove this action to its home county. (CCP § 394.) It is undisputed that the injury occurred there. San Mateo filed the motion within a reasonable time. (See Adams v. Superior Court, 226 Cal. App. 2d 365, 367 (1964).) San Mateo acted sensibly by filing this motion with its answer after the case was at issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HUSTON JACKSON VS. GETHSEMANE BAPTIST CHURCH A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion For Order That Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part. All responses to the requests for admission which provide the following equivocal answer are deemed to be denied. "Defendant does not have sufficient information or knowledge in order to admit or deny the matter. A reasonable inquiry regarding the matter has been made and the information that is known or that which is reasonably obtainable is insufficient to enable defendant to admit the matter." Requests which have been denied, including these, will be subject to post trial cost sanctions if plaintiff proves the fact is true at trial. No further responses are required to the requests for admissions and none of the requests are deemed to be admitted due to late response. Sanctions are awarded to the plaintiff, jointly and severally against the defendants for the total amount of $1,500 to be paid within 15 days from the date of this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LISA KROGER EITANI VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Compelling Answers To Discovery And For Award Of Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LISA EITANI'S Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Answers To Discovery And For Award Of Fees And Expenses Pro Tem Judge Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows. Sanctions will be awarded to the plaintiff in the amount of $500.00 payable within 15 days of this ruling. To the extent the motion is granted, full and complete responses to the discovery are due within 15 days of the date of this ruling. Those responses must clearly set forth a response by each of the defendants who have appeared in this case. - Requests for Admission: Granted as to all except numbers 8, 12, 13 and 16. Denied as to numbers 8, 12, 13 and 16. - Special Interrogatories: Granted as to all except numbers 8, 9 and 10. Denied as to numbers 8, 9 and 10. - Form Interrogatories: Granted as to 2.6, 15.1 and 17.1. Denied as to 12.1 and 50.1. - Request for Production of Documents: Granted as to 4(d). Denied as to the rest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Forprotective Order And Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 26, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. No sanctions will be awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
